body_txt: A super-power or Applied Phlebotinum device that "turns off" either all super-powers or those from a certain Meta Origin . Can also be referred to as a "power neutralizer". This can be temporary or permanent; the temporary version is generally used as either part of a Brought Down to Normal plot or as a plot device to enable villains who normally wouldn't stand a chance against the heroes, while the permanent Depowering version is usually a way to threaten someone without trying to kill them. Tailor Made Prisons for super villains usually incorporate one into the design, as do general purpose Extranormal Prisons . An environment that has this effect is a De-Power Zone . Curiously, very few people who say I Just Want to Be Normal create a modified version of these for their own personal use, even if the technology seems to be relatively available. Even worse, if a hero is Blessed with Suck , not even the smartest scientists allied with the side of Good seem able to invent a safe one. Related to Powers as Programs . For nullifying the effects of supernatural powers rather than preventing someone from using them, see Anti-Magic . If the power has already been used, Dispel Magic can remove the effects. Power Limiter is a weaker version. No-Sell is when a character ignores, not nullifies, another's powers. Mage Killers often weaponize this ability, as they do with Anti-Magic . See also the Kryptonite-Proof Suit , which can be used to resist Weaksauce Weaknesses . A type of Meta Power . Please do not confuse this trope with Anti-Magic . A laconic way of thinking about them is that this trope is a tool and Anti-Magic is an ability.     Batman The Beatles Discworld Dungeons & Dragons Harry Potter High School D×D The Hundred Line -Last Defense Academy- Marvel Cinematic Universe My Hero Academia Very common in the fandom as a whole. There’s little evidence of quirk supressants in canon but any time a character gets caught by a villain in a fanfic they’ll use either quirk suppressant cuffs or drugs. The drug version is also common in sickfics for when someone like Midoriya or Todoroki has illness related quirk effects or hallucinates. My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic A common trend in My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic fan fiction is the idea of ring or caps that can be placed on a unicorn's horn to prevent them from using spells or magical telekinesis. This is typically used either as a legal measure to restrain a unicorn's magic or as a measure to contain powerful unicorn captives. Effectiveness varies; one fic might have the device nullify any pony's magic, even the most powerful archmages and immortal alicorns. Another might have a cap of magic that such a device can neutralize, so a sufficiently angry Twilight Sparkle causes one to melt or short-circuit. Star Wars A common element in fanfiction is the idea of Force-suppressing devices used to restrain Force-sensitive captives, usally cuffs that dampen or block a person's ability to reach into the Force or a cell that completely deadens use of the Force within itself — an effect that canon only uses as the strange and exotic power of an obscure alien animal, notably. Vocaloid WinxVerse Worm
